1bbd31f1e046f81248b516f1680888634e08b0e7:
<REASONING>
From the Treasury Bulletin table “Bank Deposits of Individuals, Partnerships, and Corporations in Twenty-five Metropolitan Areas … Part A - Total Deposits”:

- New Haven percentage increase in 1943 = 17.3
- Hartford percentage increase in 1943 = 18.6

Form the first normalized two-point distribution:
P = [17.3, 18.6] / (17.3 + 18.6)

Form the second distribution by reversing the order:
Q = [18.6, 17.3] / (17.3 + 18.6)

Compute Kullback-Leibler divergence:
D_KL(P || Q) = Σ p_i ln(p_i / q_i)

Using precise calculation:
P = [0.4818941504178273, 0.5181058495821727]
Q = [0.5181058495821727, 0.4818941504178273]

D_KL(P || Q)
= 0.4818941504178273 * ln(0.4818941504178273 / 0.5181058495821727)
+ 0.5181058495821727 * ln(0.5181058495821727 / 0.4818941504178273)
= 0.00262372153147767

Rounded to 5 decimal places:
0.00262
</REASONING>
<FINAL_ANSWER>
0.00262
</FINAL_ANSWER>

fb61fe1c0ebac75f987cd75ad3fe153954e1b2eb:
<REASONING>
From the December 1990 Treasury Bulletin tables:

- September 1989 total interest-bearing U.S. public debt = 2,836,309 (million dollars)
- September 1990 total interest-bearing U.S. public debt = 3,210,943 (million dollars)

Using the corresponding “total federal debt outstanding” values for September 1989 and September 1990 from the same report:

- September 1989 total federal debt outstanding = 2,867,570
- September 1990 total federal debt outstanding = 3,233,313

Compute the ratios:

- 1989 ratio = 2,836,309 / 2,867,570 × 100 = 98.909420
- 1990 ratio = 3,210,943 / 3,233,313 × 100 = 99.308198

Absolute change in percentage points:

- |99.308198 − 98.909420| = 0.398778

Rounded to the nearest hundredth:

- 0.40
</REASONING>
<FINAL_ANSWER>
0.40
</FINAL_ANSWER>
fe047f10425af4e7e3cdfdafb652c266f900fc9c:
<REASONING>
From Treasury Bulletin June 1944, Table 1, Part A - Total Deposits:
- New Haven percentage increase during calendar year 1943 = 17.3
- Hartford percentage increase during calendar year 1943 = 18.6

Form the first two-point distribution by normalizing [17.3, 18.6]:
- Sum = 17.3 + 18.6 = 35.9
- P = [17.3/35.9, 18.6/35.9] = [0.4818941504, 0.5181058496]

Reverse the order for the second distribution:
- Q = [18.6/35.9, 17.3/35.9] = [0.5181058496, 0.4818941504]

Kullback-Leibler divergence:
KL(P || Q) = 0.4818941504 * ln(0.4818941504 / 0.5181058496) + 0.5181058496 * ln(0.5181058496 / 0.4818941504)
= 0.0026237215

Rounded to 5 decimal places:
0.00262
</REASONING>
<FINAL_ANSWER>
0.00262
</FINAL_ANSWER>
